alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Verification of specific labelling requirements is required by consulting the full regulation.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Microbiology Limits - Microbiological limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Verification of specific microbiological limits is required by consulting the full regulation.

alerts.title: Added: India Allergen List - Mandatory allergen declarations
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required against the compendium. Specific mandatory allergen declarations for seasoning, snack food, and ready-to-eat snacks must be verified against this compendium.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Additive Limits - Permitted additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited unless specified as unlikely to cause harm by MHLW. Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and traditional foods used as additives) and preparations/food containing additives are prohibited from sale, production, import, processing, use, storage, or display for sale, unless specified as safe by MHLW.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Contamination or foreign substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited if likely to cause harm due to contamination or foreign substances. Food or additives likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds are prohibited.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, and pharmaceuticals
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited if exceeding MHLW-specified quantity. Food containing residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, or pharmaceuticals exceeding the quantity specified by MHLW as unlikely to cause harm is prohibited. This forms the basis of the Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Toxic or harmful substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited if containing or bearing toxic or harmful substances. Food or additives containing or suspected to contain toxic or harmful substances are prohibited, unless the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies they are unlikely to cause harm.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - Non-conformance to MHLW established standards and criteria
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited if non-conforming to MHLW standards or criteria. Food or additives that do not conform to MHLW established standards or criteria are prohibited from production, import, processing, use, cooking, preservation, or sale.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Microbiology Limits - Diseases or abnormalities in source animals
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited for sale/use as food if from diseased/abnormal livestock/poultry. Meat, bones, milk, organs, and blood of livestock or poultry with specified diseases/abnormalities or that have died are prohibited, unless specified by MHLW Order or found safe. Imported products require certificates of sanitation.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Microbiology Limits - Pathogen contamination
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited if contaminated with pathogens likely to cause harm. Food or additives contaminated with pathogens or suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health, are prohibited.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Microbiology Limits - Spoilage
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited if rotten, deteriorated, or immature (unless proven safe). Food or additives that are rotten, deteriorated, or immature are prohibited from sale, collection, production, import, processing, use, cooking, storage, or display for sale, unless unlikely to cause harm and recognized as edible.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Compliance with regulatory limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Permitted food additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Unpermitted additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. If a food product contains an additive that is not permitted, it must not be imported or sold. An application can be submitted to SFA for evaluation.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Compliance with regulatory limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents (contaminants) in food.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants (incidental constituents)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Within SFA's regulatory limits. Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants exceeding limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. If food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed regulatory limits, they must not be imported or sold.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Veterinary drug residues (diethylstibestrol, hexoestrol, dienoestrol)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat containing residues of diethylstibestrol, hexoestrol, or dienoestrol is prohibited.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Labelling Requirement - Declaration of ingredients and additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. All ingredients and additives used in prepacked food products must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Labelling Requirement - Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish, sulphite (as residue sulphur dioxide)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Labeling required if present as ingredient or food additive. Sulphite limit: 10 or more mg/kg.. This applies to prepackaged foods containing these allergens as ingredients or food additives.

alerts.title: Added: Thailand Labelling Requirement - Source fetch / extraction issue
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Manual review required. Automated extraction failed: Expected ':' after property name in JSON at position 1909 (line 44 column 5)

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Source fetch / extraction issue
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Manual review required. Automated extraction failed: Expected ':' after property name in JSON at position 2703 (line 43 column 194)

alerts.title: Added: Codex Additive Limits - Specific food additives
alerts.details: New: Check official source. The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions for permitted food additive use in all foods. Specific provisions, including limits, are available in the online database and must be verified by food additive, functional class, and food category.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Additive Limits - Specific food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) Online Database. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: Codex Contaminant Limits - Mycotoxins
alerts.details: New: Check official source. A Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices. Compliance with this code is required to manage mycotoxin contamination.

alerts.title: Added: Codex Contaminant Limits - Specific contaminants (e.g., mycotoxins, metals like arsenic, lead, mercury)
alerts.details: New: Check official source. The Codex Alimentarius Commission establishes maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed. Specific MLs for various contaminants are detailed in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and must be verified.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and decisions by the Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF). Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Mycotoxins, metals (arsenic, lead, mercury), radionuclides
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Additive Limits - Not specified
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Verification of approved food additives and their conditions of use for seasonings and snack products is required by consulting the EU Additives Database, which is based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Additive Limits - Food additives and their conditions of use
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via database/regulation. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Not specified
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Verification of food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine) in specific foods, including seasonings and snack products, is required by consulting Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Microbiology Limits - Food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via regulation. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.details: Old: Verification required from GSO 9:2022. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.details: Old: Verification required from GSO 2055-1:2015 for specific criteria. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.details: Old: Verification required (Positive List System). Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Contaminant Limits - Contamination or foreign substances in food/additives
alerts.details: Old: Prohibited for sale. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Contaminant Limits - Ingredients of food/additives

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Contaminant Limits - Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, pharmaceuticals for animals
alerts.details: Old: Must not exceed MHLW-specified quantities. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Contaminant Limits - Toxic or harmful substances in food/additives

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - Conformance to MHLW established standards and criteria
alerts.details: New: Verification required for specific criteria and standards. The Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish criteria for methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food/additives, and standards for their ingredients. These must be verified.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - Production/processing location for specific imports
alerts.details: New: Verification required for MHLW-specified countries/regions/facilities. Food and additives specified by MHLW Order as requiring important process control measures must be imported only if produced/processed in MHLW-specified locations that take those measures.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Labelling Requirement - Sanitation management certification for imports
alerts.details: New: Verification required for certificates from exporting country's governmental organization. Food and additives specified by MHLW Order as requiring certification of food sanitation management at the production site must not be imported without attached certificates from the exporting country's governmental organization.

alerts.title: Added: Middle East (GCC/GSO) Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation specifies the general requirements for labels of prepackaged foodstuffs. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.

alerts.title: Added: Middle East (GCC/GSO) Labelling Requirement - General requirements for Halal food
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation specifies general requirements for food products to be considered Halal. Compliance is mandatory if a product is marketed as Halal. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.

alerts.title: Added: Middle East (GCC/GSO) Labelling Requirement - General requirements for unprocessed genetically modified agricultural products
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation specifies general requirements for unprocessed genetically modified agricultural products, which may impact ingredients used in seasoning, snack, and processed food products. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full standard.

alerts.title: Added: Middle East (GCC/GSO) Labelling Requirement - Mandatory shelf life periods
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for various food products. Specific periods must be verified by consulting the full standard.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Class II chemical preservatives
alerts.details: New: Verification required. If two or more Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid or benzoic acid) are used, the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives must be used to calculate permitted levels.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Specific food additive limits
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Different limits apply depending on the additive and the type of food products. Use the List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool to check if additives are allowed and their corresponding limits.

alerts.title: Removed: Singapore Additive Limits - Combined Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
alerts.details: Old: Calculation tool must be used for permitted levels. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.
